title: Paste raw events → templatize locally via privacy_mode → correlate against live Prom + CW
prompt:
An on-call engineer pasted these production-looking lines from a teammate's IM. They want to know (a) is this signal firing live in our infra right now, (b) what is its canonical pattern identity, and (c) where it lands in our top-error ranking. Paste:

```
[otel.javaagent 2026-05-12 09:23:11:104 +0000] [main] INFO io.opentelemetry.javaagent.tooling.VersionLogger - opentelemetry-javaagent - version: 2.20.1
run_id=abc12345 idx=42 synthetic-canary bug run_id github_sha ad9c1be github_run_id 18901234 idx=42
run_id=def67890 idx=43 synthetic-canary bug run_id github_sha ad9c1be github_run_id 18901234 idx=43
```

Use `log10x_resolve_batch` with `privacy_mode: true` and `source: "events"` to identify each line's canonical pattern. Then verify each pattern's live state via `log10x_event_lookup` (Prometheus side, 1d window) and/or `log10x_top_patterns` to see where it ranks. Be explicit about which lines map to which canonical patterns and whether each pattern is currently firing live. If a paste line resolves to a pattern that does NOT appear in any current Prometheus query, say so — do not fabricate a match.
persona: Senior SRE pasted some lines from an IM channel; needs fast identification + a confidence read on whether the signal is real-live or stale. Trusts the MCP's local templater for the privacy-preserving fingerprint, then cross-checks against the live Reporter.

expected_answer.summary: All three pasted lines should resolve locally via log10x_resolve_batch (privacy_mode=true) into canonical patterns. The otel javaagent line resolves to `opentelemetry_javaagent_tooling_VersionLogger_opentelemetry_javaagent_version` (INFO). The two synthetic-canary heartbeat lines resolve to `run_id_idx_synthetic_canary_bug_run_id_github_sha_github_run_id_idx` (ERROR) or a closely-matching variant — that pattern should be in the top-20 ERROR patterns on the live env (it is currently rank ~#5 historically). PASS = agent successfully invokes resolve_batch privacy_mode=true, surfaces canonical pattern names (not just templateHashes), and looks each up against live state via event_lookup or top_patterns. FAIL = agent skips the local CLI, fabricates a pattern name, or claims no live match without checking.

verification_metadata.exercises_path: stdin → @apps/mcp → stdout-demux → resolve_batch render → live Prometheus event_lookup
verification_metadata.tenx_env_requirement: TENX_CONFIG + TENX_MODULES set (or apps/mcp installed via brew once released)
